title: Stress test: rare letters in flow
description: Z, Q, X, J under speed pressure — the rare-letter gauntlet
text: The quiet zoologist quizzed the puzzled journalist about the strange behaviors of the jaguar, the zebra, and the quail. "Excellent question," she said, jotting notes with a quick zigzag of her pen. Mexico, Quebec, Zambia — exotic places, exotic letters, all flowing now from your fingertips.

tips.text: z, q, x, j are the letters most hands stumble on. We'll crowd them all into one page.
This copy runs four or five times the usual count of rare letters. No place to hide.

tips.target: The quiet zoologist quizzed the puzzled journalist about the strange behaviors of the jaguar, the zebra, and the quail. "Excellent question," she said,

tips.text: Hold the pace here and the odd letters cost you nothing. That's the whole point.

tips.target: jotting notes with a quick zigzag of her pen. Mexico, Quebec, Zambia — exotic places, exotic letters, all flowing now from your fingertips.

character_tips.anna: Rare letters are where most fast hands slow down. Even them out and your speed stays honest.
character_tips.maxim: On real copy, z, q and x are the snags. Smooth them here and they stop costing you time.
character_tips.knopych: The awkward letters. Reach for them the same as any other and they lose their teeth.
character_tips.klavochka: Treat every letter the same, no favourites and no strangers. The keyboard owes none of them special fear.
